paper: When Will It Fail? Anomaly to Prompt for Forecasting Future Anomalies in Time Series (ICML 2025)

MBA_L100.data_note: TranAD MBA has identical train/test (7680x2). 4-record SVDB run in progress.
MBA_L100.critical_finding: AUROC=0.528 near-random. Raw F1=5.35%, tolerance F1=43.1% (8x inflation)

SMD_L100.note: PID 178193, running 72+ min as of Apr 11 00:25. SMD=708K timesteps.

MBA_4rec_SVDB_L100.note: PID 179247, 4-record PhysioNet SVDB (800-803), 645K x 2, running 54+ min

no_share.note: Removing shared backbone reduces F1 (18.58% vs full 19.07%). Direction correct, magnitude differs from paper due to data issue.

no_aafn_cross_attn.note: Removing AAFN cross-attn barely changes F1 (42.55% vs full 43.1%). Near-null ablation in our setup.
no_aafn_cross_attn.caveat: Our setup uses train==test data so AAFN provides no benefit in-sample. Paper may show more effect with proper data.

mba_proper_split.description: 70/30 temporal split of MBA data (same source, no train==test overlap)
mba_proper_split.conclusion: Train==test inflates F1 by 3.4x. Even with proper split, F1 is only 12.66%. Paper uses genuine SVDB data.

grey_swan.key_finding: F1 collapses 10x from 3.12% to 0.1% anomaly rate. At 0.1%: F1=1.8%. Fundamental AP metric limitation.
grey_swan.verdict: CONFIRMED - F1 with tolerance breaks for rare events

calibration.key_finding: AUROC=0.528 (random=0.5). Raw F1=5.35%. Tolerance window inflates 8x. Brier skill=-0.12.
calibration.verdict: CRITICAL - raw scores are near-random discriminators

ltw_f1.key_finding: A2P LTW-F1=23.85% vs Random=14.25% (1.67x). Random also has high LTW/Std ratio (5.07x vs 4.46x). A2P does NOT demonstrate superior early warning.
ltw_f1.verdict: NULL - A2P does not predict more in advance than random

chronos_foundation_model_baseline.method: Chronos-Small (20M, frozen, zero fine-tuning) + forecast MSE score
chronos_foundation_model_baseline.conclusion: Zero-shot foundation model beats specialized A2P by +21.7pp AUROC. A2P's F1 gains are entirely from tolerance window, not discrimination.
caveats: ECG data may be in pretraining set

known_bugs_in_official_code: Seed hardcoded to [20462] in run.py:121, --random_seed ignored
train_loss list never appended (NaN in loss tracking, doesn't affect training)
anormly_ratio typo in argparse (kept as-is from official code)
MBA TranAD data has identical train/test (data integrity issue)

tolerance_vs_pa.finding: Tolerance t=50 == Full PA for MBA data: A2P anomaly segments are >100 steps so tolerance always captures full segment. Paper claim of limited neighborhood is equivalent to full PA on this dataset.

oracle_threshold.finding: Oracle threshold (exhaustive sweep) gives only 0.48pp over A2P actual. Score distribution is the ceiling, not threshold choice.

chronos_f1_comparison.finding: Chronos-Small (zero fine-tuning) has AUROC=0.745 but F1-tolerance=7.4%. A2P has AUROC=0.528 but F1-tolerance=43.1%. The two metrics give opposite winners - demonstrates metric fundamentally determines conclusions.
